download.title: Download ({})
field.help.backend_version: Seleziona il backend llama.cpp (CPU / Vulkan / ROCm / CUDA). Premi Invio per aprire il selettore versione. Backend diversi supportano GPU diverse e funzionalità diverse.
field.help.cache_prompt: Cache del prefisso prompt per tempi di risposta più rapidi. Quando abilitato, il prefisso prompt viene memorizzato nella cache per ridurre i calcoli ridondanti. Attiva/Disattiva con Invio.
field.help.embedding: Abilita generazione embedding. Utilizzato per ricerca vettoriale, similarità semantica e applicazioni RAG. Attiva/Disattiva con Invio.
field.help.lora: Percorso per un file adapter LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation). Applica pesi affinati senza caricare un nuovo modello. Premi Invio per selezionare un file.
field.help.lora_scaled: Percorso per un adapter LoRA con fattore di scala personalizzato. Formato: percorso:scala (es. '/percorso/a/lora.safetensors:1.0'). La scala controlla la forza dell'adapter. Premi Invio per modificare.
field.help.mmap: Mappa il file modello in memoria per un caricamento più rapido. Utilizza I/O disco per integrare la RAM, consentendo modelli più grandi. Attiva/Disattiva con Invio.
field.help.rpc: Endpoint remote procedure call per inferenza distribuita. Formato: host:porta. Lascia vuoto per operazione solo locale. Premi Invio per modificare.
field.help.webui: Abilita l'interfaccia web integrata. Attiva/Disattiva con Invio. Consente l'accesso tramite browser al server.
field.help.batch_size: Dimensione batch per valutazione (inference). Batch più grandi usano più VRAM ma possono migliorare il throughput tramite parallelismo. Valori piccoli (1-8) per VRAM bassa, grandi (16-128) per configurazioni alta VRAM.
field.help.cache_reuse: Minimum chunk size (in tokens) before KV cache is reused across requests. Higher values (e.g., 128, 256) only cache large shared prefixes, reducing disk write churn. Lower values (0-32) cache more aggressively. Adjust with Left/Right arrows (step 16).
field.help.cache_type_k: Quantization precision for KV cache keys. Lower precision (e.g., Q4, Q8) saves VRAM but may slightly reduce quality. Default is usually FP16. Use lower values if running out of VRAM.
field.help.cache_type_v: Quantization precision for KV cache values. Lower precision (e.g., Q4, Q8) saves VRAM but may slightly reduce quality. Default is usually FP16. Use lower values if running out of VRAM.
field.help.context_length: Context window size in tokens. Determines how much of the conversation history is kept in memory. A larger context allows longer conversations but uses more RAM. Typical: 32k-256k depending on model and RAM.
field.help.draft_tokens: Maximum number of draft tokens per step (0-16). More drafts = more potential speedup but also more wasted computation if drafts are rejected. Typical: 4-8 for draft-mtp.
field.help.dry_allowed_length: Number of recent tokens to check for repetition (penalty starts after this). Higher values check longer context. Typical: 2.
field.help.dry_base: DRY penalty base (log scale). Controls the strength of the repetition penalty. Typical: 1.0 (log2) or 0.0 (linear).
field.help.dry_multiplier: DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) multiplier. Scales the penalty for repetition. Higher values = stronger anti-repetition. Typical: 1.75.
field.help.dry_penalty_last_n: How many tokens to consider for DRY penalty (0 = all). Larger values catch longer repetition patterns. Typical: -1 (all) or 128.
field.help.expert_count: Number of MoE (Mixture of Experts) experts to activate per token. -1 = auto (all active). Reducing this speeds up inference for MoE models like Mixtral but may reduce quality. Typical: 2-8 for Mixtral.
field.help.fit: Automatically adjust arguments to fit device memory. Toggle on/off with Enter.
field.help.auto_chat_template: Seleziona automaticamente il template di chat dai metadati dell'architettura GGUF. Quando abilitato, llama.cpp usa un template integrato che corrisponde alla famiglia del modello (es. llama, qwen2). Richiede jinja=true. Attiva/disattiva con Invio.
field.help.chat_template: Seleziona template di chat: Auto (rileva) usa i metadati dell'architettura GGUF, i nomi dei template specifici usano i template integrati di llama.cpp, Seleziona file template scansiona ~/.config/llm-manager/chat_templates/ e tutti i suoi sottodirectories per file .jinja, Nessuno disabilita il template. Premi Invio per aprire il selettore.
dialog.chat_template.title: Template di Chat
dialog.chat_template.help: Invio per selezionare  Esc per annullare  Su/Giù per navigare
dialog.chat_template.file.title: Seleziona file .jinja
dialog.chat_template.file.help: Invio per selezionare  Esc per tornare  Su/Giù per navigare
dialog.chat_template.file.empty: Nessun file .jinja trovato nella cartella templates
field.help.flash_attn: Enable Flash Attention (flash-attn) for faster inference. Requires compatible GPU (Ampere+ / Ada). Significantly speeds up long-context inference. Only works with certain GGUF formats.
field.help.frequency_penalty: Penalizes tokens based on how often they appear in the text (+) or rewards them (-). Positive values reduce word repetition, negative values encourage denser language. Typical: 0.0 (off).
field.help.gpu_layers_mode: How many model layers to offload to GPU. Arrow keys cycle: Auto → 1 → 2 → ... → N → All → Auto. Auto lets llama.cpp decide based on VRAM. All loads every layer (999). Specific number sets exact offload count.
field.help.ignore_eos: Ignore end-of-sequence tokens during generation. Toggle on/off with Enter. Useful when you want to force the model to continue generating.
field.help.is_mtp: Speculative decoding method for faster inference. Options: Off, draft-mtp (MTP-based), draft-simple, draft-eagle3, ngram-simple, ngram-map-k, ngram-map-k4v, ngram-mod, ngram-cache. Draft-mtp requires a compatible model with MTP architecture.
field.help.keep: Number of layers to keep in memory when swapping (negative = all). Useful for fast reloading of the same model. Typical: -1 (all) or 0 (none).
field.help.kv_cache_offload: Offload KV cache to RAM when GPU memory is full. Allows larger batch sizes and contexts at the cost of some speed. Useful when VRAM is limited but you still want longer conversations.
field.help.main_gpu: Index of the main GPU (0-based). Handles initial model loading and some computations. Typical: 0 for single GPU, 0 for primary in multi-GPU setups.
field.help.max_concurrent_predictions: Maximum number of models that can run simultaneously. Press Enter to open a picker that shows how context length divides per model. Each model needs its own VRAM/CPU resources.
field.help.max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate in the response. Prevents runaway responses. Set to 0 or Disabled for no limit. Typical: 4096-8192 for chat, higher for code generation.
field.help.min_p: Minimum probability threshold relative to the most likely token. Tokens below min_p * max_prob are excluded. A filter that's more principled than top-k/top-p for controlling diversity. Typical: 0.01-0.1.
field.help.mirostat: Mirostat sampling mode: Off (default), Mirostat, or Mirostat2. Adaptive temperature control that maintains target perplexity. Mirostat2 is more aggressive. Useful for consistent output quality.
field.help.mirostat_ent: Mirostat target entropy. Controls the diversity of output. Higher = more diverse. Typical: 5.0.
field.help.mirostat_lr: Mirostat learning rate (eta). Controls how quickly the temperature adapts. Smaller = smoother adjustments. Typical: 0.1.
field.help.mlock: Lock model weights in RAM (mlock). Prevents the OS from swapping model weights to disk. Slows model load time but ensures faster inference once loaded. Useful for repeated use.
field.help.numa: NUMA (Non-Uniform Memory Access) strategy: None, Distribute, Isolate, or Numactl. Affects CPU thread affinity on multi-socket systems. None = default.
field.help.presence_penalty: Encourages the model to talk about new topics (+) or stay on topic (-). Positive values reduce topic repetition, negative values encourage deeper exploration. Typical: 0.0 (off).
field.help.repeat_last_n: How many recent tokens to check for repetition (0 = all). Smaller values (32-64) focus on local repetition, larger values (128-256) catch longer patterns. Typical: 64.
field.help.repeat_penalty: Controls repetition penalty (1.0 = no penalty, 1.1 = mild, 1.2 = strong). Higher values discourage the model from repeating phrases. Typical: 1.05-1.15 for most use cases.
field.help.rope_yarn_enabled: Enable YaRN (Yet another RoPE extensioN) for scaling context beyond training limits. YaRN uses a frequency rescaling technique to handle longer contexts. Toggle on/off with Enter.
field.help.samplers: Semicolon-separated sampler order string (e.g., 'mirostat;temperature;top_k;top_p'). Controls which samplers are applied and in what order. Press Enter to edit.
field.help.seed: Random seed for reproducible outputs. -1 = random (default). Set to a fixed value for deterministic, repeatable responses — useful for debugging or testing prompts.
field.help.spec_type: Speculative decoding method for faster inference. Options: Off, draft-mtp (MTP-based), draft-simple, draft-eagle3, ngram-simple, ngram-map-k, ngram-map-k4v, ngram-mod, ngram-cache. Draft-mtp requires a compatible model with MTP architecture.
field.help.split_mode: GPU split strategy: None, Layer (default), Row, or Tensor. Controls how model layers are distributed across multiple GPUs. Layer splits by layer count, Row/Tensor split by matrix dimensions for multi-GPU setups.
field.help.swa_full: Enable full-size sliding window attention cache. Stores complete KV entries for SWA layers instead of compressed representation. Uses more VRAM but preserves quality on very long contexts. Toggle with Enter.
field.help.system_prompt_preset_name: System prompt preset. Pre-configured prompts that shape how the model behaves (e.g., 'coder', 'assistant', 'creative'). Affects the model's personality and output style.
field.help.tags: Comma-separated labels for the model (e.g., 'coding, chat, reasoning'). Used for filtering and organization. Press Enter to open a tag editor.
field.help.temperature: Sampling temperature. Controls creativity: 0 = deterministic (most predictable), 0.7 = balanced, 1.0+ = creative. Lower values produce more focused, factual outputs. Typical: 0.7-0.9 for general use.
field.help.tensor_split: Fraction of model weights to load on each GPU (colon-separated for multi-GPU, e.g., '0.5:0.5'). For single GPU, leave empty. Press Enter to edit.
field.help.threads_batch: CPU threads for batch processing (1 to 32). Separate from Threads (inference threads). Keep equal for most workloads, or reduce batch threads to lower CPU usage during batch operations.
field.help.top_k: Only consider the top k most likely tokens at each step. Smaller top-k (e.g., 10-40) makes output more deterministic. Larger values allow more variety. Typical: 40-50. Set to 0 to disable.
field.help.top_p: Nucleus sampling: only consider tokens whose cumulative probability reaches p. Smaller top-p (e.g., 0.9) is more conservative, larger (e.g., 0.95-0.99) allows more variety. Often preferred over top-k. Typical: 0.9-0.95.
field.help.typical_p: Locally typical sampling (typ_p). Controls diversity by keeping tokens with typical probability mass. Values near 1.0 = no effect, 0.1-0.5 = moderate diversity. Typical: 1.0 (off).
field.help.ubatch_size: Unlimited batch size for prompt processing. Larger values improve prompt evaluation throughput but use more RAM. Typical: 512-2048. Set to 0 to match context_length.
field.help.uniform_cache: Share KV cache across sequences. Reduces VRAM usage when running multiple requests by reusing allocated cache. May slightly reduce performance but enables more concurrent users.
field.help.yarn_params: YaRN configuration: rope_scale (context multiplier), rope_freq_base (frequency base), rope_freq_scale (frequency scaling). Press Enter to open the YaRN parameter editor.
gguf.iq1_s: IQ1_S: Very low bit quantization (1.56 bits)
gguf.k_quant: Block-wise quantization with double-quantized scales for better quality
gguf.moe: Mixture-of-Experts: only a fraction of parameters active per token
gguf.size_m: Medium: Balanced file size and quality
gguf.size_s: Small: Smaller file, slightly lower quality
gguf.unknown_family: Unknown model family -- using generic GGUF naming conventions
gguf.unsloth_description: Dynamic bit-allocation: KL-divergence calibrated, keeps sensitive layers at higher precision

onboarding.welcome.title: Benvenuto in llm-manager
onboarding.welcome.description: Un'interfaccia terminale per gestire modelli LLM locali. Cerca, scarica ed esegui modelli GGUF tramite llama.cpp. Questo tour rapido spiega i concetti principali e i tasti rapidi. Puoi ripeterlo in qualsiasi momento con Ctrl+O.
onboarding.step1.title: Directory dei modelli
onboarding.step1.description: I tuoi file modello GGUF si trovano nella directory dei modelli (predefinita: ~/.local/share/llm-manager/models). Puoi aggiungere più directory. Posiziona i tuoi file .gguf scaricati qui e verranno scoperti automaticamente all'avvio. I modelli sono elencati nel pannello Modelli (mostra/nascondi con ^F7).
onboarding.step1.keys: `^F7` Pannello modelli · Scoperta automatica · Più directory supportate · `^G` Aiuto nome GGUF
onboarding.step2.title: Ricerca e download
onboarding.step2.description: Premi '/' per cercare modelli GGUF su HuggingFace. Esplora i risultati, visualizza il README e seleziona un modello per vedere i file GGUF disponibili. Scegli una variante (Q4_K_M è una buona scelta equilibrio qualità/velocità) e scaricala.
onboarding.step2.keys: `/` Cerca HuggingFace · `^S` Ordina · `->` Vedi README · `↵` File GGUF
onboarding.step3.title: Impostazioni del server
onboarding.step3.description: Configura il server llama.cpp: thread (usa i core fisici per prestazioni migliori), endpoint API (abilita l'API compatibile OpenAI, imposta la porta), modalità server (Normal per un singolo modello, Router per più modelli, Bench per il benchmarking), e dashboard WebSocket (abilita il monitoraggio remoto, imposta porta, chiave auth, TLS). Anche host, port, timeout, flash attention, e selezione backend.
onboarding.step4.title: Selezione del backend
onboarding.step4.description: Scegli il backend di inferenza: CPU (universale, più lento), Vulkan (GPU Intel/AMD), CUDA (GPU NVIDIA) o ROCm (GPU AMD). L'app rileva automaticamente la tua GPU e sceglie l'opzione migliore. Puoi cambiarlo in seguito nel pannello Impostazioni server.
onboarding.step4.keys: `^F8` Impostazioni server · Rilevamento GPU automatico · Modificabile in qualsiasi momento
onboarding.step5.title: Binario llama-server
onboarding.step5.description: llm-manager usa llama-server da llama.cpp per eseguire i modelli. Il valore predefinito è 'llama-server' (ricercato nel PATH). L'app può scaricare automaticamente il binario corretto per la tua piattaforma e backend GPU. Nessuna configurazione manuale necessaria.
onboarding.step5.keys: `^K` Vedi comando · Download automatico · Ricerca PATH
onboarding.step6.title: Impostazioni LLM
onboarding.step6.description: Controlla l'inferenza del modello: preset del prompt di sistema (Generale, Programmatore, Pensatore, Matematico), temperatura (creatività), top_k/top_p/min_p/typical_p (campionamento), mirostat (tasso di apprendimento adattivo), ignore EOS, lista samplers, penalità ripetizione/last N, penalità presenza/frequenza, DRY (prevenzione ripetizioni), seed, max tokens, decoding speculativo (tipo spec, draft tokens), YARN RoPE (estensione contesto), tags, e layer GPU (quanti layer da scaricare sulla GPU per il trade-off VRAM vs velocità).
onboarding.step6.keys: `^F9` Mostra/nascondi · `j/k` Naviga · `↵` Modifica · `^S` Salva · `^R` Reset · `^E` Expert
onboarding.step7.title: Pronto all'uso
onboarding.step7.description: Ora conosci le basi. Naviga tra i pannelli, carica modelli e inizia a chattare. Usa i tasti rapidi sotto. Premi Ctrl+O in qualsiasi momento per ripetere questo tour.
onboarding.step7.keys: `^F7` Modelli · `^F8` Server · `^F9` LLM · `/` Cerca · `^O` Ripeti · `^L` Lingua · `^X` Expert · `^G` GGUF
